info.title: Render Public API
info.description: Manage everything about your Render services

get.description: List Blueprints for the specified workspaces. If no workspaces are provided, returns all Blueprints the API key has access to.

post.description:
"Validate a `render.yaml` Blueprint file without creating or modifying any resources. This endpoint checks the syntax and structure of the Blueprint, validates that all required fields are present, and returns a plan indicating the resources that would be created.

Requests to this endpoint use `Content-Type: multipart/form-data`. The provided Blueprint file cannot exceed 10MB in size.
"

patch.description: Update the role of an existing workspace member.

delete.description: Remove a user from the specified workspace.

get.description: Retrieve the Blueprint with the provided ID.

patch.description: Update the Blueprint with the provided ID.

delete.description:
Disconnect the Blueprint with the provided ID.

Disconnecting a Blueprint stops automatic resource syncing via the associated `render.yaml` file. It does not _delete_ any services or other resources that were managed by the blueprint.

parameters.name: blueprintId
parameters.description: The ID of the Blueprint

get.description: List syncs for the Blueprint with the provided ID.
List dedicated IP sets for a workspace.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to list dedicated IP sets for.

post.description:
"Create a dedicated IP set. Provisioning an IP set is asynchronous. The response returns immediately with `status: CREATING` and `ips: []`. When provisioning completes, status changes to `RUNNING` and `ips` contains your assigned addresses.

If `environmentIds` is omitted or empty, this IP set applies to all services in the workspace in the selected region. Otherwise, it applies only to services in the listed environments in that region.
"

get.description: Retrieve the dedicated IP set with the provided ID.

patch.description:
"Update the dedicated IP set with the provided ID. All fields are optional. Omitted fields are left unchanged. To switch from environment-scoped to workspace-scoped, provide `environmentIds: []`.
"

delete.description: Delete the dedicated IP set with the provided ID.

parameters.name: dedicatedIpId
parameters.description: The ID of the dedicated IP set

get.description: List persistent disks matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, returns all disks you have permissions to view.

parameters.name: diskId
parameters.description: Filter by disk IDs

post.description:
Attach a persistent disk to a web service, private service, or background worker.

The service must be redeployed for the disk to be attached.

get.description: Retrieve the persistent disk with the provided ID.

patch.description:
Update the persistent disk with the provided ID.

The disk's associated service must be deployed and active for updates to take effect.

When resizing a disk, the new size must be greater than the current size.

delete.description:
Delete a persistent disk attached to a service.

**All data on the disk will be lost.** The disk's associated service will immediately lose access to it.

parameters.name: diskId
parameters.description: The ID of the disk

get.description: List snapshots for the persistent disk with the provided ID. Each snapshot is a point-in-time copy of the disk's data.

parameters.name: diskId
parameters.description: The ID of the disk

post.description:
Restore a persistent disk to an available snapshot.

**This operation is irreversible.** It will overwrite the current disk data. It might also trigger a service deploy.

Snapshot keys returned from the [List snapshots](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/list-snapshots) endpoint expire after 24 hours. If a snapshot key has expired, query the endpoint again for a new key.

parameters.name: diskId
parameters.description: The ID of the disk

get.description: Retrieve the user associated with the provided API key.
List the workspaces that your API key has access to, optionally filtered by name or owner email address.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Only return workspaces with one of the provided names. Only exact matches are returned.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Only return workspaces owned by one of the provided email addresses.

get.description:
Retrieve the workspace with the provided ID.

Workspace IDs start with `tea-`. If you provide a user ID (starts with `own-`), this endpoint returns the user's default workspace.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the user or team

get.description: Retrieves the list of users belonging to the workspace with the provided ID.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the team

get.description: Retrieve audit logs for a specific workspace with optional filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start time for filtering audit logs (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End time for filtering audit logs (ISO 8601 format)

get.description: Retrieve audit logs for a specific organization with optional filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start time for filtering audit logs (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End time for filtering audit logs (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization

get.description:
Retrieve notification settings for the owner with the provided ID.

Note that you provide an owner ID to this endpoint, not the ID for a particular resource.

patch.description: Update notification settings for the owner with the provided ID.

get.description: List notification overrides matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, returns all notification overrides for all workspaces the user belongs to.

Retrieve the notification override for the service with the provided ID.

Note that you provide a service ID to this endpoint, not the ID for a particular override.

patch.description: Update the notification override for the service with the provided ID.

get.description: List registry credentials matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, returns all registry credentials you have permissions to view.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter for the name of a credential

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Filter for the username of a credential

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter for the registry type for the credential

parameters.name: createdBefore
parameters.description: Filter for services created before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: createdAfter
parameters.description: Filter for services created after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: updatedBefore
parameters.description: Filter for services updated before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: updatedAfter
parameters.description: Filter for services updated after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

post.description: Create a new registry credential.

get.description: Retrieve the registry credential with the provided ID.

patch.description: Update the registry credential with the provided ID. Services that use this credential must be redeployed to use updated values.

delete.description: Delete the registry credential with the provided ID.

parameters.name: registryCredentialId
parameters.description: The ID of the registry credential

get.description: List services matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, returns all services you have permissions to view.

post.description: Creates a new Render service in the specified workspace with the specified configuration.

get.description: Retrieve the service with the provided ID.

patch.description: Update the service with the provided ID.

delete.description: Delete the service with the provided ID.

post.description: Trigger cache purge for the web service if caching is enabled.

get.description: List deploys matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all deploys for the service are returned.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter for deploys with the specified statuses

parameters.name: createdBefore
parameters.description: Filter for deploys created before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: createdAfter
parameters.description: Filter for deploys created after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: updatedBefore
parameters.description: Filter for deploys updated before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: updatedAfter
parameters.description: Filter for deploys updated after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: finishedBefore
parameters.description: Filter for deploys finished before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: finishedAfter
parameters.description: Filter for deploys finished after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

post.description: Trigger a deploy for the service with the provided ID.

get.description: Retrieve the details of a particular deploy for a particular service.

post.description:
Cancel an in-progress deploy for a service.

Not supported for cron jobs.

Trigger a rollback to a previous deploy of the specified service.

Triggering a rollback with this endpoint does not disable autodeploys for the service. This means an autodeploy might restore changes you had intentionally rolled back.

You can toggle autodeploys for your service with the [Update service](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/update-service) endpoint or in the Render Dashboard.

get.description: List all environment variables for the service with the provided ID.

put.description: Replace all environment variables for a service with the provided list of environment variables.

get.description:
Retrieve a particular environment variable for a particular service.

This only applies to environment variables set directly on the service, not to environment variables in a linked environment group.

put.description:
Add or update a particular environment variable for a particular service.

This only applies to environment variables set directly on the service, not to environment variables in a linked environment group.

delete.description:
Delete a particular environment variable from a particular service.

This only applies to environment variables set directly on the service, not to environment variables in a linked environment group.

get.description: List all secret files for the service with the provided ID.

put.description:
Replace all secret files for a service with the provided list of secret files.

**Any of the service's existing secret files not included in this request will be deleted.**

This only applies to secret files set directly on the service, not to secret files in a linked environment group.

get.description:
Retrieve a particular secret file for a particular service.

This only applies to secret files set directly on the service, not to secret files in a linked environment group.

put.description:
Add or update a particular secret file for a particular service.

This only applies to secret files set directly on the service, not to secret files in a linked environment group.

delete.description:
Delete a particular secret file from a particular service.

This only applies to secret files set directly on the service, not to secret files in a linked environment group.

parameters.name: envVarKey
parameters.description: The file name of the secret file

get.description: List recent events that occurred for the service with the provided ID.
List a particular service's response header rules that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all rules for the service are returned.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Filter for specific paths that headers apply to

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter for header names

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Filter for header values

post.description: Add a response header rule to the service with the provided ID.

put.description:
Replace all header rules for a particular service with the provided list.

**This deletes all existing header rules for the service that aren't included in the request.**

delete.description: Delete a particular response header rule for a particular service.

parameters.name: headerId
parameters.description: The id of the header

get.description: List a particular service's redirect/rewrite rules that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all rules for the service are returned.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter for the type of route rule

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Filter for the source path of the route

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: Filter for the destination path of the route

post.description: Add redirect/rewrite rules to the service with the provided ID.

put.description:
Replace all redirect/rewrite rules for a particular service with the provided list.

**This deletes all existing redirect/rewrite rules for the service that aren't included in the request.**

Rule priority is assigned according to list order (the first rule in the list has the highest priority).

patch.description:
Update the priority for a particular redirect/rewrite rule.

To apply redirect/rewrite rules to an incoming request, Render starts from the rule with priority `0` and applies the first encountered rule that matches the request's path (if any).

Render increments the priority of other rules by `1` as necessary to make space for the updated rule.

delete.description: Delete a particular redirect/rewrite rule for a particular service.

parameters.name: routeId
parameters.description: The id of the route

get.description: List a particular service's custom domains that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all custom domains for the service are returned.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter for the names of custom domain

parameters.name: domainType
parameters.description: Filter for domain type

parameters.name: verificationStatus
parameters.description: Filter for domain verification status (`verified` or `unverified`)

parameters.name: createdBefore
parameters.description: Filter for custom domains created before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: createdAfter
parameters.description: Filter for custom domains created after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

post.description: Add a custom domain to the service with the provided ID.

get.description: Retrieve a particular custom domain for a particular service.

delete.description: Delete a custom domain for a service given the service id and custom domain id or name.

post.description: Verify the DNS configuration for a custom domain.
Suspend the service with the provided ID.
Resume the service with the provided ID (if it's currently suspended).

Restart the service with the provided ID.

Not supported for cron jobs.

[Scale](https://render.com/docs/scaling#manual-scaling) the service with the provided ID to a fixed number of instances.

Render ignores this value as long as autoscaling is enabled for the service.

put.description: Update the [autoscaling](https://render.com/docs/scaling#autoscaling) config for the service with the provided ID.

delete.description: Delete the autoscaling config for a service given the service id.

post.description:
Create a preview instance for an image-backed service. The preview uses the settings of the base service (referenced by `serviceId`), except settings overridden via provided parameters.

View all active previews from your service's Previews tab in the Render Dashboard.

Note that you can't create previews for Git-backed services using the Render API.

get.description: List jobs for the provided service that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all jobs for the service are returned.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter for the status of the job (`pending`, `running`, `succeeded`, `failed`, or `canceled`)

parameters.name: createdBefore
parameters.description: Filter for jobs created before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: createdAfter
parameters.description: Filter for jobs created after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: startedBefore
parameters.description: Filter for jobs started before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: startedAfter
parameters.description: Filter for jobs started after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: finishedBefore
parameters.description: Filter for jobs finished before a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: finishedAfter
parameters.description: Filter for jobs finished after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

post.description: Create a one-off job using the provided service. For details, see [One-Off Jobs](https://render.com/docs/one-off-jobs).

get.description: Retrieve the details of a particular one-off job for a particular service.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The ID of the job

post.description: Cancel a particular one-off job for a particular service.

get.description: List instances for the provided service.

post.description: Trigger a run for a cron job and cancel any active runs.

delete.description: Cancel a currently running cron job.

get.description: Retrieve the details of a particular event

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The ID of the event

get.description:
List logs matching the provided filters. Logs are paginated by start and end timestamps.
There are more logs to fetch if `hasMore` is true in the response. Provide the `nextStartTime`
and `nextEndTime` timestamps as the `startTime` and `endTime` query parameters to fetch the next page of logs.

You can query for logs across multiple resources, but all resources must be in the same region and belong to the same owner.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to return logs for

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description:
The direction to query logs for. Backward will return most recent logs first.
Forward will start with the oldest logs in the time range.

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Filter logs by their resource. A resource is the id of a server, cronjob, job, postgres, redis, or workflow.

parameters.name: instance
parameters.description: Filter logs by the instance they were emitted from. An instance is the id of a specific running server.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their host. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: statusCode
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their status code. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their requests method. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: task
parameters.description: Filter logs by their task(s)

parameters.name: taskRun
parameters.description: Filter logs by their task run id(s)

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Filter logs by their severity level. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter logs by their type. Types include `app` for application logs, `request` for request logs, and `build` for build logs. You can find the full set of types available for a query by using the `GET /logs/values` endpoint.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Filter by the text of the logs. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Filter request logs by their path. [Wildcards and regex](https://render.com/docs/logging#wildcards-and-regular-expressions) are supported.

get.description:
Open a websocket connection to subscribe to logs matching the provided filters. Logs are streamed in real-time as they are generated.

You can query for logs across multiple resources, but all resources must be in the same region and belong to the same owner.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to return logs for

101.description: The connection has been upgraded to a websocket. The server will send log messages as they are generated in the format defined below.

get.description: List all values for a given log label in the logs matching the provided filters.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to return log label values for

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: The label to query logs for

get.description: Returns log stream information for the specified workspace.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to return log stream information for

put.description: Updates log stream information for the specified workspace. All logs for resources owned by this workspace will be sent to this log stream unless overridden by individual resources.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to update log stream information for

delete.description: Removes the log stream for the specified workspace.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to delete the log stream for

get.description: Lists log stream overrides for the provided workspace that match the provided filters. These overrides take precedence over the workspace's default log stream.

parameters.name: logStreamId
parameters.description: Filter log streams by their id.

parameters.name: setting
parameters.description: Filter log streams by their setting.

get.description: Returns log stream override information for the specified resource. A log stream override takes precedence over a workspace's default log stream.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: The ID of the resource (server, cron job, postgres, or redis) to return log stream override information for

put.description: Updates log stream override information for the specified resource. A log stream override takes precedence over a workspace's default log stream.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: The ID of the resource (server, cron job, postgres, or redis) to update log stream override information for

delete.description: Removes the log stream override for the specified resource. After deletion, the resource will use the workspace's default log stream setting.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: The ID of the resource (server, cron job, postgres, or redis) whose log streams should be returned

get.description: Returns metrics stream information for the specified workspace.

200.description: The metrics stream integration for the owner

put.description: Creates or updates the metrics stream for the specified workspace.

delete.description: Deletes the metrics stream for the specified workspace.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace to return metrics stream information for

get.description: Get CPU usage for one or more resources.

parameters.name: resolutionSeconds
parameters.description: The resolution of the returned data

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Resource ID to query. When multiple resource query params are provided, they are ORed together. Resources can be service ids, Postgres ids, or Redis ids

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: This parameter is deprecated. Please use `resource` instead

parameters.name: instance
parameters.description: Instance ID to query. When multiple instance ID query params are provided, they are ORed together

parameters.name: aggregationMethod
parameters.description: The aggregation method to apply to multiple time series

get.description: Get the CPU limit for one or more resources.
Get CPU target for one or more resources.
Get memory usage for one or more resources.
Get the memory limit for one or more resources.
Get memory target for one or more resources.
Get the HTTP request count for one or more resources.

parameters.name: aggregateBy
parameters.description: The field to aggregate by

get.description: Get HTTP latency metrics for one or more resources.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hosts of HTTP requests to filter to. When multiple host query params are provided, they are ORed together

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The paths of HTTP requests to filter to. When multiple path query params are provided, they are ORed together

parameters.name: quantile
parameters.description: The quantile of latencies to fetch. When multiple quantile query params are provided, they are ORed together

get.description: Get bandwidth usage for one or more resources.

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Service ID to query. When multiple service ids are provided, they are ORed together

get.summary: Get bandwidth usage breakdown by traffic source
get.description:
Get bandwidth usage for one or more resources broken down by traffic source (HTTP, WebSocket, NAT, PrivateLink).

Returns hourly data points with traffic source breakdown. Traffic source data is available from March 9, 2025 onwards.
Queries for earlier dates will return a 400 Bad Request error.

200.description: A successful response with traffic source breakdown

get.description: Get persistent disk usage for one or more resources.
Get persistent disk capacity for one or more resources.
Get the instance count for one or more resources.
Get the number of active connections for one or more Postgres databases or Redis instances.

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Resource ID to query. When multiple resource query params are provided, they are ORed together. Resources Postgres ids or Redis ids

get.description: Get seconds of replica lag of a Postgres replica.

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Postgres ID to query. When multiple resource query params are provided, they are ORed together

get.description: List instance values to filter by for one or more resources.

get.summary: List queryable status codes and host values
get.description: List status codes and host values to filter by for one or more resources.

parameters.name: statusCode
parameters.description: The status codes of HTTP requests to filter to. When multiple status code query params are provided, they are ORed together

get.description: The path suggestions are based on the most recent 5000 log lines as filtered by the provided filters
Get the total number of task runs queued for one or more tasks.

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Task ID to query. When multiple task IDs are provided, they are ORed together

get.description: Get the total number of task runs completed for one or more tasks. Optionally filter by state (succeeded/failed) or aggregate by state.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The state of task runs to filter to. When multiple state query params are provided, they are ORed together

parameters.name: aggregateBy
parameters.description: The field to aggregate by

get.description: List Key Value instances matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all Key Value instances are returned.

post.description: Create a new Key Value instance.

get.description: Retrieve a Key Value instance by ID.

patch.description: Update a Key Value instance by ID. Note that changing your plan, max memory policy, or persistence mode will restart your Key Value instance.

delete.description: Delete a Key Value instance by ID.

get.description: Retrieve connection info for a Key Value instance by ID. Connection info includes sensitive information.

post.description: Suspend a Key Value instance by ID.
Resume a Key Value instance by ID.

get.description: List Postgres instances matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all Postgres instances are returned.

post.description: Create a new Postgres instance.

get.description: Retrieve a Postgres instance by ID.

patch.description: Update a Postgres instance by ID.

delete.description:
Delete a Postgres instance by ID. This operation is irreversible, and
all data will be lost.

get.description: Retrieve connection info for a Postgres instance by ID. Connection info includes sensitive information.
Retrieve information on the availability of Postgres point-in-time recovery for a Postgres instance by ID.

post.description: Trigger [point-in-time recovery](https://render.com/docs/postgresql-backups) on the Postgres instance with the provided ID.
Suspend a Postgres instance by ID.
Resume a Postgres instance by ID.
Restart a Postgres instance by ID.
Failover a [highly available Postgres](https://render.com/docs/postgresql-high-availability) instance.

get.description: List [exports](https://render.com/docs/postgresql-backups#logical-backups) for a Postgres instance by ID. Returns a URL to download the export.

post.description: Create an [export](https://render.com/docs/postgresql-backups#logical-backups) of a Postgres instance by ID.

get.description: List PostgreSQL users for the Render Postgres instance with the provided ID.

post.description: Create a new PostgreSQL user for the Render Postgres instance with the provided ID. This becomes the database's new "default" user.

delete.description: Delete a PostgreSQL user from the Render Postgres instance with the provided ID.

get.description: List projects matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all projects are returned.

post.description: Create a new project.

get.description: Retrieve the project with the provided ID.

patch.description:
Update the details of a project.

To update the details of a particular _environment_ in the project, instead use the [Update environment](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/update-environment) endpoint.

delete.description:
Delete the project with the provided ID.

Requires _all_ of the project's environments to be empty (i.e., they must contain no services or other resources). Otherwise, deletion fails with a `409` response.

To delete a non-empty project, do one of the following:
- First move or delete all contained services and other resources.
- Delete the project in the [Render Dashboard](https://dashboard.render.com).

post.description: Create a new environment belonging to the project with the provided ID.

get.description: List a particular project's environments matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all environments are returned.
Retrieve the environment with the provided ID.

patch.description: Update the details of the environment with the provided ID.

delete.description:
Delete the environment with the provided ID.

Requires the environment to be empty (i.e., it must contain no services or other resources). Otherwise, deletion fails with a `409` response.

To delete a non-empty environment, do one of the following:
- First move or delete all contained services and other resources.
- Delete the environment in the [Render Dashboard](https://dashboard.render.com).

post.description: Add resources to the environment with the provided ID.

delete.description: Remove resources from the environment with the provided ID.

items.description: The ID of an env group, a service, a Postgres database, or a Redis instance

get.description: List environment groups matching the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all environment groups are returned.

post.description: Create a new environment group.

get.description: Retrieve an environment group by ID.

patch.description: Update the attributes of an environment group.

delete.description: Delete the environment group with the provided ID, including all environment variables and secret files it contains.

post.description:
Link a particular service to a particular environment group.

The linked service will have access to the environment variables and secret files in the group.

delete.description:
Unlink a particular service from a particular environment group.

The service will lose access to the environment variables and secret files in the group.

get.description: Retrieve a particular environment variable in a particular environment group.

put.description: Add or update a particular environment variable in a particular environment group.

delete.description: Remove a particular environment variable from a particular environment group.

get.description: Retrieve a particular secret file in a particular environment group.

put.description: Add or update a particular secret file in an particular environment group.

delete.description: Remove a particular secret file from a particular environment group.

get.description: List scheduled and/or recent maintenance runs for specified resources.

items.description: The Id of a resource that can undergo maintenance (Id of a service, a Postgres instance, or a Redis instance)

get.description: Retrieve the maintenance run with the provided ID.

patch.description:
Update the maintenance run with the provided ID.

Updates from this endpoint are asynchronous. To check your update's status, use the [Retrieve maintenance run](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/retrieve-maintenance) endpoint.

post.description:
Trigger the scheduled maintenance run with the provided ID.

Triggering maintenance is asynchronous. To check whether maintenance has started, use the [Retrieve maintenance run](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/retrieve-maintenance) endpoint.

As maintenance progresses, the run's `state` will change from `scheduled` to other values, such as `in_progress` and `succeeded`.
Create a new webhook.

get.description: List webhooks
Retrieve the webhook with the provided ID

patch.description: Update the webhook with the provided ID.

delete.description: Delete the webhook with the provided ID.

parameters.name: webhookId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the webhook

get.description: Retrieve a list of events that have been sent to this webhook, with optional filtering by timestamp.

parameters.name: sentBefore
parameters.description: Filter events sent before this time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

parameters.name: sentAfter
parameters.description: Filter for resources sent after a certain time (specified as an ISO 8601 timestamp)

get.description: List workflows that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all workflows accessible by the authenticated user are returned.

post.description: Create a new workflow service with the specified configuration.

get.description: Retrieve the workflow service with the provided ID.

patch.description: Update the workflow service with the provided ID.

delete.description: Delete the workflow service with the provided ID.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow

get.description: List known versions of the workflow service with the provided ID.

post.description: Creates and deploys a new version of a workflow.

get.description: Retrieve the specific workflow service version with the provided ID.

parameters.name: workflowVersionId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow version

get.description: List workflow tasks that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all task definitions accessible by the authenticated user are returned.

parameters.name: taskSlug
parameters.description: An array of task slugs in the format workflow-slug/task-name. An optional version can be appended (workflow-slug/task-name:version). If no version is provided, the latest version is used.

parameters.name: workflowVersionId
parameters.description: An array of workflow version IDs

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: An array of workflow IDs

get.description: Retrieve the workflow task with the provided ID.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The ID of the task

get.description: List task runs that match the provided filters. If no filters are provided, all task runs accessible by the authenticated user are returned.

parameters.name: rootTaskRunId
parameters.description: An array of root task run IDs to filter on

post.description: Kicks off a run of the workflow task with the provided ID, passing the provided input data.

get.description:
"Establishes a unidirectional event stream. The server sends events as lines
formatted per the SSE spec. Clients SHOULD set `Accept: text/event-stream`
and keep the connection open.
"

parameters.name: taskRunIds
parameters.description: Filter to a subset of task run IDs.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Must be `text/event-stream`.

get.description: Retrieve the workflow task run with the provided ID.

delete.description: Cancel a running task run with the provided ID.

parameters.name: taskRunId
parameters.description: The ID of the task run

ownerId.description: The ID of the owner for this image. This should match the owner of the service as well as the owner of any specified registry credential.

registryCredentialId.description: Optional reference to the registry credential passed to the image repository to retrieve this image.

imagePath.description: Path to the image used for this server (e.g docker.io/library/nginx:latest).

uri.description: The page to be served when [maintenance mode](https://render.com/docs/maintenance-mode) is enabled. When empty, the default maintenance mode page is served.

numInstances.description: For a *manually* scaled service, this is the number of instances the service is scaled to. DOES NOT indicate the number of running instances for an *autoscaled* service.

sshAddress.description: The SSH address for the service. Only present for services that have SSH enabled.

maxShutdownDelaySeconds.description: The maximum amount of time (in seconds) that Render waits for your application process to exit gracefully after sending it a SIGTERM signal.

maxShutdownDelaySeconds.description: The maximum amount of time (in seconds) that Render waits for your application process to exit gracefully after sending it a SIGTERM signal.

instanceId.description: When a service with a disk is scaled, the instanceId is used to identify the instance that the disk is attached to. Each instance's disks get their own snapshots, and can be restored separately.
When a service with a disk is scaled, the instanceId is used to identify the instance that the disk is attached to. Each instance's disks get their own snapshots, and can be restored separately.

sshAddress.description: The SSH address for the service. Only present for services that have SSH enabled.

maxShutdownDelaySeconds.description: The maximum amount of time (in seconds) that Render waits for your application process to exit gracefully after sending it a SIGTERM signal.

name.description: The service's name. Must be unique within the workspace.

ownerId.description: The ID of the workspace the service belongs to. Obtain your workspace's ID from its Settings page in the Render Dashboard.

repo.description: The service's repository URL. Do not specify a branch in this string (use the `branch` parameter instead).

branch.description: The repo branch to pull, build, and deploy. If omitted, uses the repository's default branch.

environmentId.description: The ID of the environment the service belongs to, if any. Obtain an environment's ID from its Settings page in the Render Dashboard.

maxShutdownDelaySeconds.description: The maximum amount of time (in seconds) that Render waits for your application process to exit gracefully after sending it a SIGTERM signal.

imagePath.description: "Must be either a full URL or the relative path to an image. If a relative path, Render uses the base service's image URL as its root. For example, if the base service's image URL is `docker.io/library/nginx:latest`, then valid values are: `docker.io/library/nginx:<any tag or SHA>`, `library/nginx:<any tag or SHA>`, or `nginx:<any tag or SHA>`. Note that the path must match (only the tag or SHA can vary)."

name.description: A name for the service preview instance. If not specified, Render generates the name using the base service's name and the specified tag or SHA.

Plan.description: The instance type to use. Legacy variants (`*_legacy`) identify grandfathered plans no longer offered for new services. Note that base services on any paid instance type can't create preview instances with the `free` instance type.

path.description: The request path to add the header to. Wildcards will cause headers to be applied to all matching paths.

twoFactorAuthEnabled.description: Whether two-factor authentication is enabled for the owner. Only present if `type` is `user`.

TeamMemberRole.description: The member's workspace role. Values are always returned in uppercase.

priority.description: Redirect and Rewrite Rules are applied in priority order starting at 0
Redirect and Rewrite Rules are applied in priority order starting at 0. Defaults to last in the priority list.
Redirect and Rewrite Rules are applied in priority order starting at 0. Moves this route to the specified priority and adjusts other route priorities accordingly.

DeployMode.description:
"Controls deployment behavior when triggering a deploy.

- `deploy_only`: Deploy the last successful build without rebuilding (minimizes downtime)
- `build_and_deploy`: Build new code and deploy it (default behavior when not specified)

**Note:** `deploy_only` cannot be combined with `commitId`, `imageUrl` or `clearCache` parameters,
as those are build related fields.
"

commitId.description:
The SHA of a specific Git commit to deploy for a service. Defaults to the latest commit on the service's connected branch.

Note that deploying a specific commit with this endpoint does not disable autodeploys for the service.

You can toggle autodeploys for your service with the [Update service](https://api-docs.render.com/reference/update-service) endpoint or in the Render Dashboard.

Not supported for cron jobs.

imageUrl.description:
The URL of the image to deploy for an image-backed service.

The host, repository, and image name all must match the currently configured image for the service.

tags.name: Audit Logs
tags.description:
[Audit Logs](https://render.com/docs/audit-logs) allow you to retrieve audit logs for workspaces and organizations.
These logs provide a trail of actions and changes made to your resources.

tags.name: Blueprints
tags.description:
[Blueprints](https://render.com/docs/infrastructure-as-code) allow you to define your resources in a `render.yaml` file and automatically sync changes to your Render services.
The API gives control over how your Blueprints are used to create and manage resources.

tags.name: Custom Domains
tags.description: [Custom Domains](https://render.com/docs/custom-domains) allow you to associate domain names with your Render services.

tags.name: Dedicated IPs
tags.description: [Dedicated IPs](https://render.com/docs/dedicated-ips) provide static, outbound IP addresses for your Render services.

tags.name: Deploys
tags.description:
[Deploys](https://render.com/docs/deploys) are the process of updating your service with new code or configuration.
These endpoints allow you to retrieve data on the deploys of your services
as well as trigger new deploys and rollbacks. Most changes to your service
will require a new deploy to take effect.

To create and manage services, use the `Services` endpoints.

tags.name: Disks
tags.description: [Disks](https://render.com/docs/disks) allow you to attach persistent storage to your services.

tags.name: Environment Groups
tags.description: Collections of environment variables and secret files that can be shared between multiple services

tags.name: Events
tags.description: View events for a service, postgres or key value

tags.name: Key Value
tags.description: [Key Value](https://render.com/docs/key-value) allows you to interact with your Render Key Value instances.

tags.name: Logs
tags.description:
[Logs](https://render.com/docs/logging) allow you to retrieve logs for your services, Postgres databases, and redis instances.
You can query for logs or subscribe to logs in real-time via a websocket.

tags.name: Maintenance
tags.description: The `Maintenance` endpoints allow you to retrieve the latest maintenance runs for your Render services. You can also reschedule maintenance or trigger it to start immediately.

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description: The `Metrics` endpoints allow you to retrieve metrics for your services, Postgres databases, and redis instances.

tags.name: Notification Settings
tags.description:
[Notification Settings](https://render.com/docs/notifications) allow you to configure which notifications you want to receive, and
where you will receive them.

tags.name: One-Off Jobs
tags.description: [One-off jobs](https://render.com/docs/one-off-jobs) are standalone tasks that run to completion using the most recent successful build of an existing service.

tags.name: Postgres
tags.description:
[Postgres](https://render.com/docs/postgresql) endpoints enable you to interact with your Render Postgres databases.
You can manage databases, exports, recoveries, and failovers.

tags.name: Projects & Environments
tags.description: Collections of services and other resources organized by environment (staging, production, etc.)

tags.name: Registry Credentials
tags.description: [Registry Credentials](https://render.com/docs/deploying-an-image#credentials-for-private-images) allow you to manage credentials for private Docker images.

tags.name: Services
tags.description: [Services](https://render.com/docs/service-types) allow you to manage your web services, private services, background workers, cron jobs, and static sites.

tags.name: Services - Cron Jobs
tags.description: [Cron Jobs](https://render.com/docs/cronjobs) allow you to interact with runs of your cron jobs.

tags.name: Services - Headers
tags.description: Use HTTP headers to inject response headers in static site responses. You can also use wildcards like /path/* to add headers to responses for all matching request paths.

tags.name: Services - Routes
tags.description: Add Redirect or Rewrite Rules to modify requests to your site without writing code. You can use URL parameters to capture path segments, and wildcards to redirect everything under a given path.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: The `User` endpoints allow you to retrieve information about the authenticated user

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description: The `Workspaces` endpoints supply more information about the workspaces that your API key has access to. This category was previously called `Owners`, as reflected by endpoint paths.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: [Webhooks](https://render.com/docs/webhooks) allow you to manage your Render webhook configuration.

tags.name: Workflows (Beta)
tags.description: Create and manage [Render Workflows](https://render.com/docs/workflows) services. Workflows are in public beta.

tags.name: Workflow Tasks (Beta)
tags.description: Run and manage tasks as part of [Render Workflows](https://render.com/docs/workflows). Workflows are in public beta.

tags.name: Redis (Deprecated)
tags.description: [Redis](https://render.com/docs/redis) allows you to interact with your Render Redis instances. This API is deprecated in favor of the Key Value API.
